Blue Biotech:
Policy: Nagoya Protocol access & benefit-sharing; bioresource permitting and compliance; bioethics governance
Sociology: bioprospecting ethics; benefit-sharing with provider communities; governance of traditional knowledge; commodification debates
Living Resources (Fisheries/Aqua):
Policy: fisheries management rules (quotas/catch controls); monitoring-control-surveillance (MCS) frameworks; licensing
Sociology: commons governance; fisher compliance trust; small-scale vs industrial power asymmetries; livelihood security; gendered division of labour
R&I (Research & Innovation):
Policy: open science regimes (FAIR data); science-policy interface for blue governance; research ethics and integrity
Sociology: knowledge co-production; participatory governance; boundary work between science and policy; responsible research and innovation
Renewable Energy (Wind/Ocean):
Policy: marine renewable energy policy; MSP spatial allocation/de-confliction; grid integration planning
Sociology: energy justice; social licence to operate; sea-space conflicts & co-use; community benefit-sharing; just transition in coastal regions
Non-Living Resources (Mining):
Policy: mineral licensing; (where applicable) International Seabed Authority 'Mining Code'; geological assessment incl. carbon sequestration (CCS) considerations
Sociology: extractivism & political ecology; uncertainty and risk governance; global-commons justice; distributional impacts; rights of affected communities
Desalination:
Policy: water governance & permitting; brine discharge rules; water-energy nexus planning within coastal catchments
Sociology: water justice/affordability; techno-politics of infrastructure; public acceptance & risk perception; equity impacts on coastal communities
Maritime Transport:
Policy: IMO convention framework; EU decarbonization rules incl. FuelEU Maritime; routeing/efficiency governance
Sociology: global labour regimes (flags of convenience); shipboard social order; fatigue and safety culture; automation effects on seamanship; welfare and repatriation risks
Port Activities:
Policy: port governance and port-city policy; hinterland connectivity planning; land-use coordination at the port-city interface
Sociology: port-city interface conflicts; dock labour relations & casualization; environmental justice (air/noise); stakeholder governance; security vs openness
Ship Repair & Shipbuilding:
Policy: ship recycling and hazardous-materials rules (Hong Kong Convention and related regimes); green retrofit standards; compliance documentation
Sociology: occupational health and safety; subcontracting and precarious work; shipbreaking community impacts; 'just transition' in industrial regions
Coastal Tourism:
Policy: local coastal tourism strategy; visa/mobility governance; marine spatial planning (MSP) + integrated coastal zone management (ICZM) for land–sea interactions (LSI)
Sociology: tourism mobilities; coastal community livelihoods; seasonal/service labour; place-making & heritage; social carrying capacity
Maritime Defence:
Policy: EUROSUR situational awareness; CISE cross-authority maritime surveillance information-sharing; integrated border management
Sociology: securitization; humanitarian borderwork/search & rescue; maritime migration governance; civil-military relations; accountability/legitimacy
Infrastructure & Robotics:
Policy: technical standards for offshore/subsea systems; permitting & safety cases; seabed/subsea mapping for infrastructure siting
Sociology: socio-technical systems; human-automation interaction; deskilling/reskilling; responsibility & auditability in autonomous systems; offshore work cultures

Interpretation rule for reading every cell below:

Sociology: the key maritime-social-science “problem field” that should shape learning outcomes (labour relations, conflict, justice, values and interests, emotions and traditions, identity, securitization, community impacts, etc.).
